uses: Dortmanna dioeca, also known as the Marsh St. John's Wort or Marsh-wort, is a plant with potential medicinal uses. Historically, it was used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and fever. Its roots and rhizomes contain compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
